---
title: "Week 4: Tidy and join tables"
author: "Andrew Heiss"
date: "2026-02-17"
---

## General tips

Check out [tidyexplain](https://www.garrickadenbuie.com/project/tidyexplain/) for helpful animations of R's different joining functions

---

There are a few ways you can disable or comment out entire chunks:

1. Comment out every line

```{r}
#| echo: fenced
# 1 + 1
# mean(1:5)
# median(1:5)
```

2. Use `echo: false` to hide it and `eval: false` to make it not run:

```{{r}}
#| echo: false
#| eval: false

1 + 1
mean(1:5)
median(1:5)
```

3. Turn the chunk into regular, non-executable text with `{.r}` instead of `{r}`:

```` markdown
```{.r}
1 + 1
mean(1:5)
median(1:5)
```
````

## Recreation start

```{.r}
mdrd_tidy <- mdrd %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = starts_with("diet"),
    names_to = "treatment",
    values_to = "value"
  ) %>%
  filter(value == 1) %>%
  select(-value) %>%
  group_by(treatment, ptid) %>%
  summarize(
    gfr_slope = (last(gfr) - first(gfr)) / (last(months) - first(months))
  ) %>%
  left_join(supp, by = join_by(ptid))
```
